records.mapped_scope: How a pharmacologically relevant species is determined.
records.analyst_rationale: Candidate amendment-synthesis slice, source-faithful to the reviewed Module 3.3 mapping ich_s6_r1.amend.001 (REV-005) for the same endpoint pair: Part I 3.3 defines a relevant species (pharmacological activity via receptor/epitope expression) and states that a variety of techniques can identify one. Part II 2.1 explains how that determination is made in practice: target sequence homology as a starting point, in vitro binding/occupancy comparisons, and functional-activity assessment. The Addendum records elaborate on and disambiguate the evidence used to satisfy the Parent definition; they do not change it, so the relation is clarifies rather than a broadening or narrowing of scope.
records.original_relationship_wording: No explicit source-text connective phrase (for example an explicit 'as described in' cross-reference) links these Addendum records to Parent 3.3; both the source_record_ids and amending_record_ids endpoints are reproduced verbatim from the reviewed source-layer pilot, and the relationship is an analyst-derived reading of the Addendum's elaboration on species-relevancy determination introduced generally in the Parent.
